title: Higher energy prices might eat your tax refund, economists say
description: What the taxman giveth, the energy shock taketh away. title: USS Gerald R. Ford ‘operational’ after laundry fire injures sailors
description:
A fire broke out in the main laundry area of the USS Gerald R. Ford (CVN 78) while the aircraft carrier operated in the Red Sea as part of Operation Epic Fury, according to a statement from the US Naval Forces Central Command/US 5th Fleet.
The post USS Gerald R. Ford operational after laundry fire injures sailors appeared first on Naval Technology.

title: Analysis: Naim Qassem says Hezbollah will continue fighting Israel, frames war as defense of Lebanon
description:
Hezbollah Secretary-General Naim Qassem gave his second speech regarding the renewed war with Israel, once again arguing that his group was the victim of aggression and not the aggressor. He said that Hezbollah is fighting a war of self-defense on behalf of Lebanon and stressed that it would continue fighting until it secures adequate concessions from Israel.
The post Analysis: Naim Qassem says Hezbollah will continue fighting Israel, frames war

title: Dozens of airstrikes target Iran-backed militias across Iraq
description:
The unclaimed airstrikes, likely conducted by the United States as part of US-Israeli operations against the Islamic Republic of Iran, have targeted several Tehran-allied militias in Iraq and reportedly caused significant casualties. The US has not commented on these operations, which include over 30 reported strikes on the groups, who continue to attack US forces in the region. 
The post Dozens of airstrikes target Iran-backed militias across Ir

title: Iran-backed Iraqi militias claim downing of US aircraft, US says not due to ‘hostile fire’
description:
The Islamic Resistance in Iraq, a front organization for Iranian-backed militias, claimed to have shot down a US Air Force KC-135 Stratotanker over western Iraq but provided no evidence. US Central Command denied that the aircraft was shot down, and reports indicated that it had collided with another US warplane. 
The post Iran-backed Iraqi militias claim downing of US aircraft, US says not due to ‘hostile fire’ appeared first on FDDs Long War Jo

title: International shipping 'chaos' as Strait of Hormuz closure sends ripple effects across the globe
description: Around 90% of global goods are transported by sea. And while just approximately 5% of that travels directly through the Strait of Hormuz, Iran's effective closure of the key passage is having a ripple effect, disrupting trade routes world wide. FRANCE 24's Sharon Gaffney speaks with Ebru Surucu-Balci, Assistant Professor in Circular Supply Chains at the School of Management at the University of Bradford.

title: Qatar orders departure of two Iranian diplomats over gas facility attack
description: Qatar's foreign ministry on Wednesday ordered Iran's military and security attaches along with their staff to leave the country within 24 hours, following an attack on its massive natural gas facility. FRANCE 24's Hoda Abdel-Hamid reports from Doha.

title: Are US soldiers really "refusing orders" to fight in Iran war?
description: More than two weeks after the US and Israel launched military strikes on Iran, a rumour has sprung up that American soldiers are refusing, en masse, to fight. There is no evidence for it. However, the number of conscientious objectors in the US military is on the rise, according to the Centre on Conscience and War NGO.

title: Why Hungary's Viktor Orban is vilifying Ukraine before crucial elections
description: Weeks before crucial parliamentary elections, Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban’s campaign has characterised his main rival as a stooge of Kyiv and Brussels. With his party trailing in the polls, the nationalist leader has sought to escalate tensions between Hungary and Ukraine, alleging that Kyiv and its European allies are conspiring to drag Hungary into war with Russia.

title: Liverpool thrash Galatasaray 4-0 to reach Champions League quarterfinals
description: Liverpool book quarterfinal with champions Paris Saint-Germain after dismantling Turkish giants Galatasaray at Anfield.

title: 10 major Iranian naval targets struck in Operation Epic Fury
description:
President Donald Trump has identified the Iranian Navy as a major target in the US campaign against the Islamic Republic of Iran. US forces have struck a range of port infrastructure and key vessels to degrade its capability, destroying more than 100 vessels by March 16.

title: Israeli strikes eliminate Iranian security chief Larijani and Basij commander Soleimani
description:
Overnight Israeli strikes in Iran killed Ali Larijani, the secretary of Iran's Supreme National Security Council, and Basij commander Gholamreza Soleimani. The eliminations add to the growing list of top Iranian officials who have been killed by Israeli and American strikes since the conflict began on February 28.

Simultaneously, the Tehran regime has publicly projected stability after Mojtaba Khamenei’s

title: Purported Iran-backed group claims responsibility for attacks in Belgium and Greece
description:
A previously unknown organization called the Islamic Movement of the Companions of the Right claimed responsibility for an explosion that targeted a synagogue in Liège, Belgium, and another unconfirmed attack at an unknown location in Greece this week. It is unclear if the organization is authentic, but recent Iranian threats aimed at European countries over the war could indicate a connection to the Islamic Republic.

title: Senegal calls for inquiry after being stripped of AFCON title
description: In tonight's edition, Senegal is demanding a corruption investigation, after the Confederation of African Football stripped the Lions of Teranga of their 2025 Africa Cup of Nations title. Also, Nigerian President Bola Tinubu meets King Charles and the royal family at Windsor Castle for a 2-day visit. And we learn more about "the right of salt" -- an age-old custom in North Africa honouring women during Ramadan.

title: Israel kills Iran’s intel chief as both sides ratchet up attacks on energy facilities
description: Iran suffered major blows on Wednesday with the killing of its intelligence minister, Esmail Khatib, in an Israeli strike and an attack on the South Pars natural gas field, one of the world's largest gas resources and a pillar of the country's energy supplies. Tehran vowed retaliation for the attacks, targeting facilities in Saudi Arabia, Qatar, Oman, Kuwait, Bahrain and the UAE.

title: US seeks to curb soaring petrol prices
description: The Trump administration is taking steps to curb rising energy costs, as it continues to wage war in the Middle East. The US is easing sanctions on Venezuela, and suspending a century-old shipping law, in an effort to get more oil supplies into the global market. Oil prices continues to rise, with crude topping $109 per barrel, as Gulf energy infrastructure was targeted by strikes. Also in the show - the UN's maritime agency seeks safe passage fo

title: 'Acute risk' that Iraq could become once again victim of internal conflict as Iran war progresses
description: Speaking with FRANCE 24's Yinka Oyetade, Zaid Al-Ali, Visiting Fellow at Princeton University and Former UN Advisor in Iraq, explains that until the war in Iran broke out, Iraq had been able to "straddle between US-aligned interests and Iranian-aligned interests" but there's an "acute risk" that it will become the victim of internal conflict once again as the war progresses.

title: Iran did not try to rebuild enrichment after June 2025 attack, US intel chief says
description: Iran made no effort to rebuild uranium enrichment after its capabilities were destroyed in a June 2025 US-Israeli attack, US intelligence chief Tulsi Gabbard testified Wednesday, contradicting President Donald Trump's justifications for his ongoing war. FRANCE 24's Fraser Jackson reports from Washington.

title: Kharkiv's opera and ballet theatre performances continue despite war
description: Ukraine’s second-largest city, Kharkiv, is just 30km from Russia and suffers almost daily bombardment from across the border. But as the years of war have gone by, the city’s cultural institutions have adapted in order to keep working despite the dangers. title: Both sides in Iran war ratchet up attacks on energy facilities, as oil prices surge

title: What to know about the resignation of Joe Kent, counterterrorism chief and Trump loyalist

title: To return to Gaza, Palestinian refugees first have to face Israeli-backed militias
description: Israel has said it is reopening the Rafah border crossing between the Gaza Strip and Egypt on Wednesday. Israeli authorities had partially opened the crossing on February 2 as part of the peace plan agreed with Hamas in October, but closed it on February 28 when Israeli and US missiles and planes began striking Iran.

title: Beirut building collapses amid fresh Israeli strikes in capital city's centre
description: #Israel led some of the most intense strikes against #Lebanon's capital centre since the start of the offensive on March 2. At least a dozen died, bringing up the number of people killed since early March to at least 900.

title: Trump pressures media on Iran war with claims of 'treason'
description: US President Donald Trump and his allies are attacking news organisations for their coverage of the war on Iran, a conflict the administration says is going well for the United States but is unpopular with the public and has plunged the Middle East into chaos. Trump accused unspecified “fake news media” of working with Tehran to spread AI-generated images and said they should be charged with treason.

title: Tens of millions more in acute hunger if Mideast war drags on, UN warns
description: An extra 45 million people could face acute hunger if the Mideast war carried on through June, swelling the number worldwide to a "terrible" high, the United Nations warned Tuesday. The war, now in its third week, has killed hundreds and seen Iran launch retaliatory strikes against Israel and Gulf nations, as well as a front opening in Lebanon with Israel battling Hezbollah.

title: Cuba vows 'unbreakable resistance' as US pressure mounts
description: Cuba's leader on Tuesday said the US would face "unbreakable resistance" if it tries to take over the impoverished island nation, as communist authorities scrambled to fix a nationwide electricity blackout. Cuba's government is under increasingly crushing pressure, with Washington enforcing an oil blockade and openly stating it wants to end the nearly seven-decade-old US standoff with the one-party communist state.

title: Zelensky on European tour to urge allies not to forget Ukraine
description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky on Wednesday was making his visit to Spain after a stop in London on Tuesday seen as part of an effort by Kyiv to keep the war against Russia a priority for European capitals, even as they grapple with fall-out from the US-Israeli war against Iran that is consuming the Middle East.

title: Syrian refugees flee Israeli offensive in Lebanon, returning to a Syria in ruins
description: At the Joussieh border crossing near the Syrian city of Homs, thousands of Syrian refugees are fleeing the devastating war in Lebanon between Israel and Hezbollah. Driven out by Israeli air strikes after years of exile in Lebanon, families and children wait for hours before returning to a country where 90 percent of the population is surviving in post-Assad poverty. FRANCE 24's Dana Alboz and Hussam Hammoud report.

title: Former Columbia University president Lee C. Bollinger on the importance of free speech
description: The former president of Columbia University in New York, which was at the centre of sometimes violent protests against the war in Gaza, has spoken to FRANCE 24 about the importance of free speech and how universities have to teach it. Lee C. Bollinger is president emeritus of Columbia University; he ran it until just a year before the protests broke out. The university has long been known as a bastion of freedom of expression in the United States

title: Top US official resigns over Trump's war, says Iran posed no imminent threat
description: Joe Kent, the director of the National Counterterrorism Center, announced his resignation on Tuesday, citing his concerns about the justification for military strikes in Iran and saying he “cannot in good conscience” back the Trump administration’s war. “Iran posed no imminent threat to our nation, and it is clear that we started this war due to pressure from Israel and its powerful American lobby,” Kent said in a statement posted on social media

title: Europe Has Helped the U.S. Protect Shipping. The Strait of Hormuz Is Different.
description: European Union ministers and leaders pushed back on ideas to protect the strait, including expanding a maritime operation already in place in the Red Sea.

title: Israel Intensifies Strikes on Beirut, Escalating Its Campaign Against Hezbollah
description: The Israeli military widened its attacks to the districts in the center of the Lebanese capital, destroying buildings, forcing residents to flee and killing at least 10.

title: At Least 23 Killed in Nigeria as Insurgent Attacks Go On Despite U.S. Help
description: A teaching hospital and two markets in the city of Maiduguri were hit in what a military spokesman said were suicide bombings by Boko Haram.

title: Sucked Into War, Gulf Countries Face the Limits of U.S. Security Guarantees
description: Barraged by Iranian attacks and questioning the value of security ties with the United States, nations in the Gulf have turned to Ukraine, Australia and Italy for help.

title: Израильская армия призвала всех гражданских лиц покинуть юг Ливана
description: Армия обороны Израиля (ЦАХАЛ) предупредила гражданское население южного Ливана о необходимости эвакуироваться к северу от реки Захрани, сообщает в среду The Times of Israel."Воздушные удары продолжаются, поскольку ЦАХАЛ ведет активные боевые действия в этом районе.

title: Российским бадминтонистам отказали в участии в чемпионате Европы
description: Европейская конфедерация бадминтона (Badminton Europe) отказалась приглашать российских спортсменов на чемпионат Европы. Читать далее

title: Аудиторы USAID выявили нарушения в надзоре за помощью Украине
description: Аудиторы USAID выявили нарушения в надзоре за $26 млрд помощи Украине. Читать далее

title: Аудиторы USAID выявили нарушения при предоставлении помощи Украине
description: Аудиторы Агентства США по международному развитию (USAID) во время проверки обнаружили нарушения в предоставлении Украине финансовой помощи. Об этом сообщила газета The New York Post со ссылкой на отчет.

title: ‘We’re playing Russian roulette’: People with disabilities struggle to reach bomb shelters
description:
Of the 600,000 severely disabled and elderly in Israel, about 40% — roughly 240,000 — cannot make it to safety as missiles fall from Iran and Lebanon, says Access Israel chairman
The post Were playing Russian roulette: People with disabilities struggle to reach bomb shelters appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: Russia sharing satellite imagery and drone technology with Iran – report
description:
Moscow said providing its own domestically produced components to improve strike capabilities of Iranian Shahed drones; White House says support not making any difference to war
The post Russia sharing satellite imagery and drone technology with Iran  report appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: How the Iran war is also unleashing an economic world war
description: Countries across South Asia are imposing emergency measures like rationing energy, closing universities, cutting short workweeks and even changing the way crematoriums work to deal with the fallout from the Iran war.Why it matters: Yes, the war is raising gas prices for Americans and causing a political headache for President Donald Trump — but it's also creating a deeper crisis abroad that governments and businesses are scrambling to manage.The

title: Nvidia's race to outpace physics
description: Nvidia's chips are improving at such a staggering pace that it defies any historical comparison.Why it matters: Without these gains — which are drawing increased attention as AI transforms society — physics would slam the brakes on the data center boom.Driving the news: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang said Monday he expects the company to reap "at least" $1 trillion in revenue for its newest chips through 2027.It posted record sales and earnings last mon

title: Israel Says it Destroyed Iran’s Air Force One
description: The Israeli Air Force claims it has destroyed the Airbus A340-313 used by Iranian officials, including Khamenei, which was at Tehrans Mehrabad ​airport. The Israeli Air Force says it has destroyed Iran’s “Air Force One” aircraft, an Airbus A340-313 which was used by senior Iranian officials ​and military leaders to travel both domestically and internationally.
